title: dalbergia coromandeliana
intro: Dalbergia coromandeliana, also known as the Coromandel ironwood or Indian black wood, is a tree species native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Dalbergia coromandeliana include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat skin conditions such as wounds, cuts, and eczema. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared into herbal teas, decoctions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
